description: Assuming the compiler vectorized a loop because optimization flags were set, when aliasing or dependencies forced scalar code.
body: Failure mode: -O3 and a target ISA are enabled and the loop is declared 'vectorized', but pointer aliasing, an unknown trip count, or a loop-carried dependency made the compiler emit scalar code; the expected speedup never materializes and nobody checked. Detection signals: no speedup despite -O3; vectorization remark says 'not vectorized: possible aliasing'; disassembly shows scalar ops. Do not assume vectorization from flags alone. Safer fallback: read the vectorization remarks, inspect the emitted assembly for packed instructions, remove the blocker (restrict, known bounds), and confirm the speedup tracks lane width.
